title: Dog Vomiting Bright Green Liquid: Causes and Emergency Guide
meta_desc: Seeing bright green liquid when your dog vomits? Learn why this is a high-priority emergency and what immediate steps you need to take.
content_html:
What Does Bright Green Vomit Mean?

When a dog vomits bright green liquid, it usually indicates the presence of bile, a digestive fluid produced by the liver. However, if the color is exceptionally vibrant or neon, it may be a sign that your dog has ingested something dangerous, such as rodenticide (rat poison) or items containing industrial dyes. It is important to distinguish between simple grass-stained vomit and a true liquid discharge.

Urgency Level: High

Seeing bright green vomit is considered a high-priority medical emergency. This symptom can be linked to life-threatening conditions including toxin ingestion, severe gallbladder issues, or a high-level gastrointestinal blockage. Because certain poisons act quickly on the central nervous system or blood clotting abilities, you should contact an emergency veterinarian or a pet poison control center immediately.

Why Taking a Photo Helps Triage

While it may feel unpleasant, taking a clear photo of the vomit is incredibly helpful for the veterinary team. The exact shade—whether it is forest green, neon, or yellowish-green—can provide clues to the source. A photo allows the vet to see the consistency and check for any fragments of bait or packaging, which helps them prioritize your dog's treatment the moment you arrive at the clinic.

faqs.question: Could it just be grass?
faqs.answer: While grass can turn vomit green, it usually contains visible blades of grass and a more organic scent. Bright, neon, or purely liquid green vomit is much more likely to be bile or a chemical toxin.

faqs.question: Is it okay to wait until my regular vet opens?
faqs.answer: No. Because bright green vomit can indicate the ingestion of fast-acting toxins like rat poison, waiting even a few hours can be fatal. Seek emergency care immediately.

faqs.question: What should I tell the emergency vet on the phone?
faqs.answer: Tell them the exact color, when the vomiting started, any other symptoms like lethargy or shaking, and if you have any known poisons or chemicals in your home or yard.
